description: Trigger discrimination: unattributed time/memory investigation design (this skill) vs functional root-cause (systematic-debugger), an evidenced single query (query-plan-reader), chatty data access (n-plus-one-detector), standing telemetry (observability-operator), and pass/fail measurement (performance-test-harness).

cases.prompt: Everything about the export job feels slow but nobody can say whether it's the queries, the transformation code, or writing the output — design how we find out where the time goes.
cases.reason: No evidenced suspect exists — the ask is the attribution methodology itself: profiler class per layer, measurement conditions, narrowing loop. Nothing is functionally broken, no single query is named, and no standing telemetry is requested.

cases.prompt: The nightly job produces wrong totals about once a week — walk the evidence and find the root cause.
cases.note: SEAM CASE — 'find the root cause' with a BEHAVIOR defect (wrong output) is functional debugging. The profiling skill owns resource/time attribution only; wrong totals are not a where-does-time-go question.
cases.reason: Correctness defects take the hypothesis/reproduction loop of systematic-debugger; no time or memory attribution is involved.

cases.prompt: We already know it's this one report query — here's its EXPLAIN ANALYZE output. Tell us what's wrong and how to fix it.
cases.reason: The suspect is evidenced and singular with plan output in hand. Profiling methodology first would be ceremony — the profiling skill's own stop condition routes evidenced suspects directly to the owning skill.

cases.prompt: The dashboard page fires about 600 nearly identical queries per load according to the query log. Diagnose and design the fix.
cases.reason: Hundreds of near-identical queries per request is the chatty-data-access signature — pattern diagnosis and batching/eager-load design, not time attribution and not single-plan reading.

cases.prompt: Instrument the checkout service with latency histograms and traces, and build the dashboard so we can watch performance continuously.
cases.note: SEAM CASE — continuous instrumentation and dashboards are STANDING telemetry (observability-operator, manual-invocation). Profiling is a bounded, targeted investigation that may CONSUME that telemetry as input.
cases.reason: The deliverable is permanent instrumentation and dashboards, not a bounded attribution investigation with a stop rule.
